source: classic real-world Java pitfall (java.text.SimpleDateFormat is documented as not thread-safe)
scope: TimestampFormatter.format() + the shared static FMT field

expected_bug.summary: SimpleDateFormat is mutable and NOT thread-safe (it keeps a Calendar as internal state). Sharing one static instance across concurrent request threads causes interleaved access to that state, producing corrupted/wrong timestamps or ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException under load. Looks like a harmless caching optimization.
expected_bug.l_code_hint: L7 (concurrency — shared mutable state across threads)

fixed_change: Switch to java.time.DateTimeFormatter, which is immutable and thread-safe, so the shared static field is safe.
